name: Security Testing

x.defectClass: Exploitable vulnerabilities: injection, broken auth/access control, misconfiguration, vulnerable dependencies, secrets exposure.
x.oracle: Security requirements / threat model and known vulnerability classes (e.g. OWASP ASVS, CWE) — a finding is a violated control or exploitable flaw.
x.automationLevel: Mixed — SAST/DAST/SCA automate broadly; manual pentest finds logic/auth flaws tools miss.
blindSpots: Coverage limited to known vulnerability classes
False positives drown signal if untuned
x.cost: Medium–high — tooling plus expert manual assessment.
x.lifecycleStage: Across the SDLC: SAST at build, DAST/pentest pre-release, continuous SCA & DAST in CI.

def: Testing aimed at finding exploitable weaknesses — combining static analysis (SAST), dynamic scanning (DAST), dependency scanning (SCA), and manual penetration testing against a threat model.
problem: Functional tests confirm features work; they do not reveal how the system can be abused or attacked.
mechanism: Layered: SAST inspects source for unsafe patterns, DAST attacks the running app, SCA flags vulnerable dependencies, and human pentesters probe auth, access control, and business logic. Findings are triaged against a threat model and standards like OWASP ASVS.
benefits: Combines automation breadth with expert depth
limits: Manual testing is expensive and point-in-time
whenNot: Never skip for internet-facing / sensitive systems
As the only control (it complements secure design)
myths: "A passing scanner means the app is secure" — scanners cover known classes, not logic or design flaws
